cards.prompt: A worker thread's write to a shared field is visible after Join; a new Thread is a foreground thread by default (IsBackground False)
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; int result = 0; var t = new Thread(() => { result = 40 + 2; }); bool bg = t.IsBackground; t.Start(); t.Join(); Console.WriteLine($"result={result} background={bg}");

cards.prompt: A dedicated new Thread is not a thread-pool thread (False); a ThreadPool.QueueUserWorkItem callback runs on a pool thread (True)
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; bool a=false, b=false; var t=new Thread(()=>{ a = Thread.CurrentThread.IsThreadPoolThread; }); t.Start(); t.Join(); var done=new ManualResetEventSlim(false); ThreadPool.QueueUserWorkItem(_=>{ b = Thread.CurrentThread.IsThreadPoolThread; done.Set(); }); done.Wait(); Console.WriteLine($"dedicated_pool={a} poolthread_pool={b}");

cards.prompt: lock makes each increment a critical section; joining all 8 threads before printing makes the total deterministic: 8 * 1000 = 8000
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading; long total = 0; object gate = new object(); var ts = new Thread[8]; for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) ts[i] = new Thread(() => { for (int j = 0; j < 1000; j++) lock (gate) total += 1; }); foreach (var t in ts) t.Start(); foreach (var t in ts) t.Join(); Console.WriteLine($"total={total}");
